NAME: UK Biobank family-based GWAS (Guan et al.)
MAIN_ANCESTRY: EUR (unified / Young et al. in White British); multi-ancestry robust estimator
TRAIT: 19 UKB phenotypes (education, cognition, income, health, anthropometrics, lipids, etc.); genome-wide sumstats for height, BMI, and educational attainment; chromosome 22 SNPs for remaining traits
DESCRIPTION: Family-based GWAS (FGWAS) summary statistics estimating direct genetic effects (DGEs) with unified, robust, Young et al. Mendelian-imputation, and sib-difference estimators implemented in snipar. Unified estimator adds singletons via linear parental imputation (largest DGE effective sample size in homogeneous samples); robust estimator avoids allele-frequency imputation bias under strong structure or admixture. UK Biobank application (n up to ~408k unified White British; ~52k robust with ≥1 genotyped first-degree relative).
CITATION: Guan J, Tan T, Nehzati SM, Bennett M, ...&, Young AS. (2025) Family-based genome-wide association study designs for increased power and robustness. Nat Genet, 57 (4) 1044-1052. doi:10.1038/s41588-025-02118-0. PMID 40065166
